item.title: Design Patterns Brown-Bag Series
item.description: A series of brown-bag talks I'm giving at the Johns Hopkins Applies Physics Lab on Design Patterns. (Added session 2)

item.title: Design Patterns Brown-Bag Series
item.description: A series of brown-bag talks I'm giving at the Johns Hopkins Applies Physics Lab on Design Patterns. (Added session 1)

item.title: Android Intents and Fragments
item.description: A tech talk I gave for Johns Hopkins University on Oct 6, 2014 on Android Intents and Fragments. (I had to rush the Fragments section b/c I didn't realize the talk was only supposed to be one hour... Sigh...)

item.title: Java 8 - Lambdas and Streams - Columbia MD Java Users Group, Laurel, MD
item.description: A JUG talk from September 2014 I presented on new features in Java: Lambdas and Streams.

item.title: Effective Eclipse - Columbia MD Java Users Group, Laurel, MD
item.description: A JUG talk from May 2010 I presented on Eclipse tips and tricks.

item.title: Java Enumerations - APL Java Users Group, Laurel, MD
item.description: A JUG talk from May 2009 I presented on enumerations in Java.

item.title: Template Method and Strategy in Java - Columbia MD Java Users Group
item.description: New to Design Patterns? Learning Template Method and Strategy is a good starting point! This is from a lecture I gave on 10/13/2011 for the Columbia, Maryland Java Users Group

item.title: Installing the Android Development Tools
item.description: This video walks through setup of the Android Development tooling in Eclipse and a simple "Hello World" application

item.title: Dynamic Proxies in Java - Columbia MD Java Users Group
item.description: Everything you ever wanted to know about Dynamic Proxies in Java but didn't know to ask. This is from a lecture I gave on 12/02/2010 for the Columbia, Maryland Java Users Group

item.title: Annotations Part 6 - Code Generation - JavaBean boilerplate example
item.description: This example demonstrates how boilerplate code needed for JavaBeans can be generated. This is from a lecture I gave on 1/19/2010 for the Columbia, Maryland Java Users Group

item.title: Annotations Part 5 - Code Generation - RMI interface example
item.description: This example demonstrates how an RMI interface can be generated from an annotated basic interface. This is from a lecture I gave on 1/19/2010 for the Columbia, Maryland Java Users Group

item.title: Annotations Part 4 - Runtime Annotations - UI action setup example
item.description: This example demonstrates how annotated methods can be automatically set up as ActionListeners in a user interface. This is from a lecture I gave on 1/19/2010 for the Columbia, Maryland Java Users Group

item.title: Annotations Part 3 - Runtime Annotations - Unit test example
item.description: Example of annotations that are loaded at runtime and used by reflective code. The example is simple unit tester ala junit. This is from a lecture I gave on 1/19/2010 for the Columbia, Maryland Java Users Group

item.title: Annotations Part 2 - Classfile Annotations
item.description: Creation of custom annotations that stay in the classfile. This is from a lecture I gave on 1/19/2010 for the Columbia, Maryland Java Users Group

item.title: Annotations Part 1 - Introduction
item.description: Overview of annotations. This is from a lecture I gave on 1/19/2010 for the Columbia, Maryland Java Users Group

item.title: ANTLR 3.x tutorial Part 9: Interpreting Expressions using an ANTLR TreeWalker
item.description: Yet another way to interpret the expressions uses ANTLR's built-in tree-generation and tree-walker grammars.

item.title: ANTLR 3.x tutorial Part 8: Interpreting Expressions using the GoF Interpreter Pattern
item.description: Here we interpret the expressions using the "Interpreter" Design Pattern from the Gang-of-Four's "Design Patterns" book.

item.title: ANTLR 3.x tutorial Part 7: Interpreting Expressions while parsing the grammar
item.description: Let's try interpreting expressions, so we can find out what 3 + 4 * 2 + 5 should be.

item.title: ANTLR Concepts
item.description: What is ANTLR? What is parsing? What is a translator? A compiler?

item.title: ANTLR 3.x tutorial Part 6: XL Recognizer continued
item.description: Part 6 of creating a recognizer for XL in ANTLR 3.x

item.title: ANTLR 3.x tutorial Part 5: XL Recognizer continued
item.description: Part 5 of creating a recognizer for XL in ANTLR 3.x

item.title: ANTLR 3.x tutorial Part 4: XL Recognizer continued
item.description: Part 4 of creating a recognizer for XL in ANTLR 3.x

item.title: ANTLR 3.x tutorial Part 3: XL Recognizer continued
item.description: Part 3 of creating a recognizer for XL in ANTLR 3.x

item.title: ANTLR 3.x tutorial Part 2: XL Recognizer continued
item.description: Part 2 of creating a recognizer for XL in ANTLR 3.x

item.title: ANTLR 3.x tutorial Part 1: XL Recognizer
item.description: Part 1 of creating a recognizer for XL in ANTLR 3.x

item.title: Creating and Executing an ANTLR 3.x Grammar in Eclipse
item.description: Let's create and execute a dummy grammar just to see the mechanics in Eclipse.

item.title: Setting up ANTLR 3.x in Eclipse
item.description: How do we set up Eclipse for developing ANTLR 3.x parsers?
